summary: Matched a block of gm_1B03 game-mode functions, mostly around camera mode, training, stamina/event/menu scene transitions, and CSS/SSS/start-melee data plumbing. The PR replaced several placeholder stubs with real C, refined function prototypes and shared structs, converted magic major/minor scene IDs to named enums, and corrected nested gmMainLib data access after identifying an EventData substructure at offset 0x530. The decomp.dev report credited 13 new full matches and one partial improvement, increasing matched code by 2312 bytes.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed gm_1B03 functions: gm_801B2298_OnInit, gm_801B23F0, gm_801B24B4, gm_801B254C, gm_801B25D4, gm_801B26AC, gm_801B2704, gm_801B2790 partially, gm_801B91C8, gm_801B927C, gm_801B931C, gm_801B9560, gm_801BAA60, and gm_801BAAD0.
Added gmcamera.h usage so StartMeleeData rule callbacks can reference gmCamera_801A31FC, gmCamera_801A3098, and gmCamera_801A30E4.
Changed gm_80165268 from void(void) to void(int unused), matching callback-style use in StartMeleeData rules x38 and x3C.
Corrected gm_801B0730's color pointer parameter from s8* to u8*, consistent with PlayerInitData color being updated to u8.
Updated PlayerInitData.color in src/melee/mn/types.h from signed s8 to unsigned u8, removing casts at call sites that passed player color values.

Introduced a nested struct EventData inside gmMainLib_804D3EE0's gmm_x0 layout at offset 0x530, replacing raw padding and direct unk_535/unk_584/unk_588 accesses with gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_530 fields.
Moved gm_80473814's extern declaration for TrainingMenuData into gm_1B03.static.h and expanded TrainingMenuData to include PlayerInitData players[6] at offset 0x14.
Replaced many magic major/minor scene IDs with named constants such as MJ_CHALLENGER_APPROACH, MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_CAMERA_MODE, MJ_TITLE, MJ_MENU, MN_MENU, and MN_DEBUG_MENU.
Updated gmscdata.c to use MJ_CAMERA_MODE for a major scene entry instead of raw 0x0A.
Refined lbdvd.h by giving lbDvd_800174BC an explicit void parameter list.
smart_moves: The struct work in gm/types.h made several later matches cleaner by recognizing offset 0x530 as EventData instead of continuing to address scattered fields directly on gmMainLib_804D3EE0.
Replacing numeric scene IDs with MajorSceneKind and named enum constants improved readability without changing behavior and made switch cases in gm_801B0FF8 self-documenting.
Fixing PlayerInitData.color to u8 aligned the type with call-site behavior and eliminated forced signed/unsigned casts in gm_801B1B74.
The callback prototype fix for gm_80165268 explains why an otherwise unused parameter is needed: it is installed into game rule callback slots.
Use of #pragma dont_inline and PAD_STACK in gm_801BAA60 documents matching-sensitive compiler-shaping decisions rather than hiding them in unexplained code.
Forward-declaring struct EventData in gm_1B03.h allowed gm_801BA938's prototype to become more specific without requiring the full type in the public header.
decomp_lessons: When multiple unrelated-looking unknown fields sit near the same offset in a global game-mode state object, try grouping them into a local substructure; later functions may reveal that the region is a mode-specific data block.
Scene-transition code benefits from typing temporary variables as MajorSceneKind and using MJ_/MN_ constants; this can preserve matching while making control-flow intent searchable.
Unsigned small fields matter: a one-byte color field should be u8 when values are copied through CSS, VsModeData, and PlayerInitData without sign semantics.
Callback slots may require otherwise-unused parameters. Match prototypes should follow how the function is stored and called, not just how its body uses arguments.
Moving extern declarations into the relevant static/internal header can reduce ad hoc externs inside C files while preserving symbol visibility for matching work.
Partially matched large functions can still be valuable if their surrounding struct and prototype corrections unlock many smaller exact matches.
naming_conventions: Major scene constants use MJ_ names, e.g. MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_CAMERA_MODE, MJ_MENU, MJ_TITLE, MJ_STAMINA_VS, and MJ_CHALLENGER_APPROACH.
Minor/menu constants use MN_ names, e.g. MN_MENU and MN_DEBUG_MENU.

Mode-specific slices of gmMainLib_804D3EE0 remain offset-named when their semantic names are not fully known, e.g. unk_950, unk_D10, unk_10D0, but comments annotate likely modes such as training mode, stamina melee, fixed camera mode, and single button melee.
Newly identified but still not fully named event state is represented as struct EventData with x-offset-style fields such as x2, x3, x44, and retained inferred names like unk_535 and unk_588.
Function names remain address-based gm_801Bxxxx where no retail/source name is known, with lifecycle suffixes such as _OnInit and _OnLoad preserved.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_801B0730 and gm_801BAA60 to preserve matching-sensitive inlining behavior.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in gm_801BAA60, indicating stack layout padding was needed for the generated code to align with the target.
Changed types rather than adding casts where evidence supported it, notably u8 color and MajorSceneKind var_r0.
Kept an unused formal parameter in gm_80165268 to match callback ABI expectations.
Matched DVD preload/snapshot logic in gm_801B23F0 using GXGetTexBufferSize, OSRoundUp32B, lbDvd_80017740, lbSnap_8001E204, and lbSnap_8001E210.
Reused existing gm_801A427C/gm_801A4284 scene data accessors to recover CSSData, SSSData, and StartMeleeData plumbing.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev reported matched code for GALE01 increased by 0.06%, plus 2312 bytes.

decomp.dev reported 13 new full matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1B03: gm_801B931C, gm_801B2298_OnInit, gm_801B25D4, gm_801B23F0, gm_801B927C, gm_801B2704, gm_801B254C, gm_801BAA60, gm_801BAAD0, gm_801B91C8, gm_801B24B4, gm_801B26AC, and gm_801B9560.
decomp.dev reported gm_801B2790 improved from 0.00% to 90.37% but was not fully matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match gm_801B2790 after this 90.37% partial improvement.
Search for additional uses of gmMainLib_804D3EE0->unk_530 to refine struct EventData field names.
Check whether the temporary inline struct in gm_801B927C was later replaced with a named SSSData-like type.
Search for other PlayerInitData fields where signedness corrections remove casts and improve matches.
Look for later renames or semantic comments for VsModeData blocks unk_950, unk_D10, and unk_10D0.
